question: How fo I create a citation
reply:
Hello @, and welcome to Wikipedia! I see that you mostly use the [POLICY], and it’s a lot easier to create a citation with VisualEditor than the wikitext editor.
To create a citation, click the Cite button (the one that looks like quotation marks), then choose to
Automatically generate a citation using a URL, ISBN (or other identifier)
Manually fill out a citation template
Reuse a citation already in the article
I prefer manually filling out, but automatic citations are usually sufficient. Remember that everything in Wikipedia [POLICY] with [POLICY]. If you have more questions, don’t hesitate to ask me using your or at the [POLICY]. Happy editing! ~~

question: Hello, how do I create a citation?
reply:
Sean J. Prescott Good day! When citing web sources on Wikipedia, it's best to use [TEMPLATE_REF], and for books, use [TEMPLATE_REF]. You can also enable the [POLICY] to add citations easily to articles. With VisualEditor, simply copy and paste the URL into the citation box and click "create." However, if the author's name isn't in the usual place (typically the top left corner of the article), VisualEditor might not capture it, and you'll need to add it manually.
I recommend going through [POLICY] (The Wikipedia Adventure) and exploring the tutorial sections of the "Welcome message", which can be found in the top left corner of your Talk page. This will help you get familiar with fundamental guidelines and helpful instructions.
Feel free to ask if you have any further questions! Stay safe and best.

question: Hello How do I create a citation?
reply: Hello LynnOConnorF, and welcome to Wikipedia. I'm sorry i've been a little slow in answering; i hope this will help. There are a couple of page you can look at to help with adding references, you can fine them at [POLICY] and especially [POLICY] and [POLICY]. For me, the easiest way to add a citation is to use a pair of X tags and then use a CS1 template between the tags (where i have put "X"). You can learn about these templates at [HELP_PAGE]; the simplest to use is usually [CITATION] which requires an URL and website and title, as explained in the HELP link i gave. question: Hello, how do I publish my draft?
reply:
Hi Shugg670987 and welcome to Wikipedia. Sorry for the delay, it was thanksgiving for me yesterday and I was spending time with friends. I assume you're talking about [DRAFT]? [POLICY] explains a lot about drafts if you're curious, but I've added a template for you that has a button you can press to submit the draft for someone else to review.
Reading [POLICY] about creating new articles may be informative. While it sounds like Ormsby makes interesting Minecraft parody songs, to have an article on Wikipedia he would have to meet our [POLICY]. The best way to ensure that is to have three sources that are independant and [POLICY] that talk about his work [POLICY]. It may be the case that you cannot find sources like this no matter how hard you try. While that can definitely be disappointing, there's still plently else you could do here. There's a wikiproject dedicated to improving YouTube-related topics: [POLICY]. Maybe you'd find it interesting? There's also [POLICY].
If you need me to clarify anything or any other questions, feel free to come back here and ask whenever.

question: Hello! How do I publish a draft?
reply: Mattdimarc, well at the moment your draft has been put up for review, which could take a little bit, so be patient. However, your draft as it is currently will not be accepted, because it does not show that you are [POLICY]. To fix that, you will need high quality [POLICY]. I have just declined your draft to that effect. At the top will be the decline reasons. You should follow some of the blue links and learn more about the process. Also, you should realize that your draft is written like an advertisement. Wikipedia is not for promotion. Our articles are written [POLICY], in an encyclopedic style that does not puff up the subjects. There is definitely a lot to take in here, so don't be afraid to post followup questions :)
